mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as corporate earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in months with RSAFS growth above 0.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions, which reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% in a single month
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows that in triggered instances, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive at least 40% of the time or average return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low and inflation is rising, real interest rates become negative, signaling overheating and imminent Fed tightening, which depresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Conditional on FEDFUNDS < 2% and CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative in at least 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime with unemployment already below 4%, further declines in the unemployment rate indicate labor market tightness that compresses corporate margins and leads to negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4% and declines further, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative, or the signal is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A month-on-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals a contraction in real economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as earnings downgrades materialize.
failure_condition: In months following an INDPRO decline > 1%, the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases or the average return exceeds zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above 1.5 million units per year signals strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which boosts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon through positive sentiment and real estate sector gains.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not greater than the unconditional average return over the same period.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year inflation exceeding 3% erodes consumer purchasing power and forces the Fed to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in at least 40% of instances or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals a healthy expansion in aggregate output, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as the economic backdrop improves.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is below 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window.
